title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 3rd June 2026 || TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Not for short term; long-term hold with good potential
recommendations.detail: On a viewer holding 2,500 shares at 215 for a one-month horizon, Kutumba Rao said one shouldn't buy this for the short term at all. He recalled it went to 280-290 last Sep-Oct then fell to 145, rose again to 230 with a higher top. He advised a trading stop-loss to limit losses and said a big rally would only come if it crosses 250 again, which may not happen easily; short-term holders can sell into a small rally, but it is a good long-term (1-3 year) hold.

recommendations.note: Hold long term; book partial profits and trail stop if bought lower
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao advised holding Good Luck India for the long term ('lock-down'), noting results were not as great as expected but the stock has held up at higher levels (touched 1,400+, now around 1,300). If bought at lower levels, book partial profits and put a trading stop. He said the low P/E supports continuing to hold for the long term.

recommendations.note: No risk; SEBI penalty is routine, company doing well
recommendations.detail: On the recent SEBI report/penalty (~Rs 28 crore), Kutumba Rao said there is no risk and advised holding. He noted the penalty has already been appealed to SAT and such cases are routine (compared it to an old Reliance Petrochemicals case dismissed in court). For a company posting quarterly profit above Rs 2,000 crore, a ~Rs 20 crore SEBI notice is nothing; the company is doing well, so hold.

recommendations.note: Not a stock to watch daily; hold high-priced quality name
recommendations.detail: For a viewer holding 12 shares of Honeywell Auto bought at 37,000, Kutumba Rao said it is not a share to look at daily and advised simply holding. He noted it is a high-priced share that has frequently given good returns over time and is not a cheap stock either.

recommendations.note: Put in the locker; review after a year or two
recommendations.detail: For a viewer at a 6% loss asking hold or sell, Kutumba Rao said once you buy MRF you should put it in the locker and look at it after a year or two; it is not a stock to track daily.

recommendations.note: Won't buy despite discount; competition up, weak results
recommendations.detail: On whether to buy Bata at a discount, Kutumba Rao asked why it is available cheap, pointing to rising competition and results not coming as great as expected. While peers like Relaxo and other footwear stocks rose a bit, Bata is still close to its 52-week low (around 650-660). He said he would not buy it; better to buy Bata's footwear than the share now.

recommendations.note: Can't withstand competition; near 52-week low, could fall to 250
recommendations.detail: A viewer planned to invest Rs 1 lakh each in Cello World and MV Photovoltaic on the thesis that China-import restrictions would benefit them. Kutumba Rao said that reasoning is not correct: Cello World cannot withstand competition and has been making lower-tops/lower-bottoms since listing. Even after a somewhat impressive recent quarter, the stock sits near its 52-week low, and expecting it to rise on an unrelated macro development is foolishness; he feels Cello World could touch even 250.
